claim: In adult tissue, most T cells are memory T cells.

result.claim: In adult tissue, most T cells are memory T cells.
result.reasoning: The claim is directly supported by evidence from paper ID 23460562, titled "Early life compartmentalization of human T cell differentiation and regulatory function in mucosal and lymphoid tissues". The abstract of this paper explicitly states: "Whereas adult tissues contain a predominance of memory T cells, in pediatric blood and tissues the main subset consists of naive recent thymic emigrants...". This directly confirms that in adult tissues, memory T cells are the main subset. While the paper has 0 citations and an unknown publication year, which limits its credibility, the statement is clear and unambiguous.

evidence.text: Major tissue-resident macrophage populations maintain themselves during adulthood independent of replenishment by blood monocytes in mice.
evidence.source: Fate mapping reveals origins and dynamics of monocytes and tissue macrophages under homeostasis.

evidence.text: B cells interact with helper T cells during humoral immune responses.
evidence.source: Finding the right niche: B-cell migration in the early phases of T-dependent antibody responses.

evidence.text: Delayed T cell recovery and restricted T cell receptor diversity after allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation are associated with increased risks of infection and cancer relapse.
evidence.source: Quantitative assessment of T-cell repertoire recovery after hematopoietic stem cell transplantation

evidence.text: Mouse studies indicate that tissue localization of T cell subsets is important for both protective immunity and immunoregulation.

evidence.text: T-zone chemokines guide activated B cells to the T zone.

evidence.text: Effector memory T cells (T(EM)) were found only in the lungs and small intestine of pediatric subjects.
evidence.source: Early life compartmentalization of human T cell differentiation and regulatory function in mucosal and lymphoid tissues

evidence.text: Regulatory T (T(reg)) cells comprise 30-40% of CD4(+) T cells in pediatric tissues.

evidence.text: In adult tissues the main subset of T cells are memory T cells.

evidence.text: Between 1986 and 1992, 44 cases of adult T cell leukaemia/lymphoma were diagnosed in the United Kingdom.
evidence.source: HTLV-I/II associated disease in England and Wales, 1993-7: retrospective review of serology requests.

evidence.text: The study explored how adult stem cells remodel chromatin in response to activating cues using mouse skin.
evidence.source: Genome-wide maps of histone modifications unwind in vivo chromatin states of the hair follicle lineage.

evidence.text: Sox2-GFP transgenic mice were generated to characterize the properties of adult neural stem cells (NSCs).
evidence.source: Cell Stem Cell Article In Vivo Fate Analysis Reveals the Multipotent and Self-Renewal Capacities of Sox2 + Neural Stem Cells in the Adult Hippocampus

evidence.text: During influenza infection, memory CD4+ T cells lead to early control of virus.
evidence.source: Memory CD4+ T cells induce innate responses independently of pathogen

evidence.text: Memory CD4+ T cell–induced IICs and viral control are optimal when memory cells are either T helper type 1 (TH1) or TH17 polarized.
evidence.source: Memory CD4+ T cells induce innate responses independently of pathogen

evidence.text: Memory CD4+ T cells enhance production of multiple innate inflammatory cytokines and chemokines (IICs) in the lung.
evidence.source: Memory CD4+ T cells induce innate responses independently of pathogen

evidence.text: T cell activation is predicated on the interaction between the T cell receptor and peptide-major histocompatibility ligands.
evidence.source: The stimulatory potency of T cell antigens is influenced by the formation of the immunological synapse.

evidence.text: Antigen-specific CD8+ T-cell tolerance is induced by myeloid-derived suppressor cells (MDSCs).
evidence.source: Altered recognition of antigen is a mechanism of CD8+ T cell tolerance in cancer

evidence.text: Adult T cell leukaemia/lymphoma was first described in 1977.
evidence.source: HTLV-I/II associated disease in England and Wales, 1993-7: retrospective review of serology requests.

evidence.text: CD4(+)CD25(+) regulatory T cells (Tregs) from individuals with a range of human autoimmune diseases are deficient in their ability to control autologous proinflammatory responses when compared with nondiseased, control individuals.
evidence.source: Type 1 diabetes-associated IL2RA variation lowers IL-2 signaling and contributes to diminished CD4+CD25+ regulatory T cell function.

evidence.text: Regulatory T (T(reg)) cells are present at much lower frequencies (1-10%) in adult tissues.
evidence.source: Early life compartmentalization of human T cell differentiation and regulatory function in mucosal and lymphoid tissues

evidence.text: B cells differentiate into low-affinity plasma cells or high-affinity plasma cells and memory B cells.
